# Grand Funding LLC

Grand Funding LLC is a direct private hard money lender licensed in Arizona and California. They offer fast-close real estate investment loans — no income docs, no W-2s, no tax returns. Approval is asset-based: the property value and deal quality determine eligibility, not the borrower's credit file or employment history.

## Key Facts

- Loan range: $70,000 – $5,000,000
- States licensed: Arizona and California
- Approval turnaround: 24-hour term sheet after submission
- Funding speed: 3–5 business days (purchases/bridge), 7–12 days (refinances), 5–10 days (construction)
- Max LTV: 75% on bridge loans and refinances
- Max ARV: up to 90% ARV on fix-and-flip loans
- Max CLTV: 75% on second position loans
- No income documentation required (asset-based lending)
- No prepayment penalty on most loans
- Direct lender — in-house decisions, no broker middleman
- NMLS ID: 2466872 | AZ MLO License: 1048901
- Phone: (602) 935-0371
- Email: info@grandfundingllc.com
- Website: https://www.grandfundingllc.com

## Loan Products

- Fix-and-Flip Loans: up to 90% ARV, interest-only, 6–12 month terms for Arizona investment property purchases and renovations
- Bridge Loans: up to 75% LTV, 3–5 day close, 6–24 month terms for time-sensitive acquisitions
- Construction Loans: up to $5M, milestone-based draws, ground-up new builds and major renovations
- Second Position Loans: up to 75% CLTV, add a junior lien behind an existing first mortgage to access equity without refinancing
- Cash-Out Refinance: up to 75% LTV on investment properties, replace existing mortgage with a larger loan and receive the difference in cash

### About & Trust
- /about — About Logan Sullivan, NMLS #2466872, and Grand Funding LLC
- /faq — Frequently asked questions about hard money lending

## Frequently Asked Questions

**What is a hard money loan?**
A hard money loan is an asset-based short-term loan secured by real property. The lender's decision is based on the collateral value (the property), not the borrower's income, employment, or credit score. Used primarily by real estate investors for acquisitions, renovations, bridge financing, and equity extraction.

**How fast can Grand Funding close a loan in Arizona?**
Grand Funding issues term sheets within 24 hours of submission. Purchase and bridge loans typically close in 3–5 business days. Cash-out refinances close in 7–12 business days due to payoff coordination. Construction loans close in 5–10 business days. The main variable is title company speed, not lender speed.

**What types of loans does Grand Funding offer?**
Fix-and-flip loans (up to 90% ARV), bridge loans (up to 75% LTV), construction loans with milestone draws (up to $5M), second position loans (up to 75% CLTV), and cash-out refinance for investment properties (up to 75% LTV).

**Do I need to show income or tax returns?**
No. Grand Funding is an asset-based lender. No W-2s, no tax returns, no pay stubs, no income verification required. Approval is based on property value and deal quality.

**What states does Grand Funding lend in?**
Arizona and California. Arizona coverage includes Phoenix, Scottsdale, Tempe, Mesa, Gilbert, Chandler, Tucson, and statewide. California coverage includes Los Angeles, San Diego, and statewide.

**What is the minimum and maximum loan amount?**
$70,000 minimum to $5,000,000 maximum.

**Is Grand Funding a direct lender or a broker?**
Grand Funding is a direct private lender — they fund loans from their own capital and make underwriting decisions in-house. No broker, no middleman, no committee approval required. This is why they can issue term sheets in 24 hours and close in days.

**What is a second position loan?**
A second position loan (also called a junior lien or second mortgage) is a new loan placed behind an existing first mortgage. The existing first mortgage stays in place. Used by investors who have a low-rate first mortgage they want to keep — the second position loan lets them access equity without refinancing.

**What is the difference between a cash-out refinance and a second position loan?**
A cash-out refinance pays off the existing first mortgage and replaces it with a larger new loan — you receive the difference in cash. A second position loan adds a new loan behind your existing first, which remains unchanged. If you have a low-rate first mortgage (3–5% range), a second position loan lets you pull equity without losing that rate.

**How do I apply for a hard money loan with Grand Funding?**
Apply online at grandfundingllc.com/apply, call (602) 935-0371, or email info@grandfundingllc.com. Grand Funding responds the same day with a term sheet or follow-up questions.

**What is LTV and how does it affect my loan?**
LTV (Loan-to-Value) is the loan amount divided by the property value. Grand Funding lends up to 75% LTV on bridge loans and refinances. For fix-and-flip loans, they lend up to 90% of ARV (After Repair Value) — the projected value after renovation.

**What is ARV?**
ARV stands for After Repair Value — the estimated market value of a property once renovations are complete. Fix-and-flip loans are sized against ARV, not the current as-is value. Grand Funding pulls comparable sales to validate ARV before issuing a term sheet.

## Founder & Direct Lender

Logan Sullivan is the founder and direct lender at Grand Funding LLC. He personally reviews every deal — there are no call centers, no layered approvals, and no committee underwriting. When you call Grand Funding, you talk to the person who decides whether your deal funds.

- **Logan Sullivan** — Founder & Direct Lender
- NMLS ID: 2466872
- AZ MLO License: 1048901
- 40+ years of experience in real estate, lending, and development
- Former builder and developer himself — understands what investors actually need: speed, certainty, and a straight answer
- Direct line: (602) 935-0371
- Email: info@grandfundingllc.com

## Ideal Borrower Profile

Grand Funding is a fit for:
- Real estate investors with a deal under contract or a property to refinance
- Fix-and-flip projects in Arizona or California (acquisition + rehab)
- Bridge financing between a sale and a purchase
- Ground-up construction (residential or small commercial) with a build budget and timeline
- Cash-out refinance on existing investment properties
- Borrowers who need to close faster than a conventional bank can
- Foreign nationals, self-employed, and credit-challenged borrowers (asset-based, not credit-based)
- Repeat investors who value relationship lending over rate-shopping

Grand Funding is NOT a fit for:
- Owner-occupied primary residences (they only lend on investment / business-purpose property)
- Loans below $70,000 or above $5,000,000
- Borrowers who need 30-year amortization (loans are short-term, typically 6-24 months)
- Properties outside Arizona or California (with rare exception for nationwide deals)

## Why Investors Choose Grand Funding

1. **Speed**: 24-hour term sheets, 3-5 day funding on purchases. Most hard money lenders take 7-14 days.
2. **Direct lender**: Logan funds the loan, decides on the loan, and is the contact for the loan. No middleman to slow things down.
3. **Asset-based**: No income docs, no tax returns. The property and the deal qualify the loan.
4. **Real conversations**: Logan picks up the phone. Investors with active deals appreciate this — most lenders return calls in 1-3 business days.
5. **Repeat-borrower friendly**: Logan knows his repeat clients by name. Future deals close even faster because the relationship is already established.

## Recent Funded Deals (snapshot)

- Payson, AZ — Bridge loan — $250,000 — Closed in 48 hours
- Scottsdale, AZ — 2nd Position — $650,000 — Closed in 3 days
- San Diego, CA — 2nd Position — $100,000 — Closed in 3 days
- Goodyear, AZ — Land — $130,000 — Closed in 7 days
- Honolulu, HI — Construction — $2,000,000 — Ground-up residential
- Gainey Ranch, AZ — Fix & Flip — $950,000 — Rehab funding

## How to Reach Logan Today

The fastest path is the phone: **(602) 935-0371**. Logan answers calls personally during business hours and returns missed calls within an hour during the workday. If you're calling about a deal already under contract, mention that — those get prioritized.

For investors who prefer email or are outside business hours: **info@grandfundingllc.com**. Most emails are returned within 24 hours.

The pre-approval form at **grandfundingllc.com/apply** takes 60 seconds and triggers an immediate review. Logan responds with a term sheet (or follow-up questions) within 24 hours, often the same day.
